steps.title: Recognize the implicit comparison
steps.content: London Array's generating capacity (about 630 MW) is presented first, then Hornsea Project One is introduced as having a generating capacity of 1,218 MW. Placing two farms with different capacities back-to-back signals that the values should be compared.

steps.title: Select the transition expressing comparison
steps.content:
The logical relationship is contrast: London Array's 630 MW capacity is placed beside Hornsea Project One's 1,218 MW capacity to show the scale difference. The blank needs a transition signaling that side-by-side comparison.
  • Choice A — ‘As a result,’: signals cause-effect, so fails because Hornsea's capacity is not caused by London Array's capacity. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘That is,’: signals restatement or clarification, so fails because Hornsea's figure is a new, different fact. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘By comparison,’: signals a direct comparison between two measured quantities, so fits. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘For instance,’: signals a specific example of a general claim, so fails because no general claim precedes the Hornsea sentence. ✗
This confirms C is correct.
